summaries.title: 2013_PMC4515582_Are_Recent_Medical_Graduates_More_Skeptical_of_Vaccines
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A cross-sectional survey investigating the association between healthcare provider graduation year and their beliefs regarding vaccine efficacy, safety, and disease severity.
key_findings: Recent graduates had 15% decreased odds of believing vaccines are efficacious.
Recent graduates had lower odds of believing childhood vaccines were safe.
3.7% of recent graduates believed immunizations do more harm than good.
summaries.population: 551 primary care providers

summaries.title: 2014_PMC5779422_Human_papillomavirus_vaccination_coverage_among_adolescents_2007
summaries.ehr_evidence: Questionnaire mailed to providers to obtain vaccination history from medical records.
summaries.summary: Analysis of HPV vaccination coverage among US adolescents (13-17 years) from 2007-2013 using the National Immunization Survey-Teen (NIS-Teen).
key_findings: Vaccination coverage increased significantly from 2012 to 2013 for both girls and boys.
Missed vaccination opportunities were common.
summaries.population: Adolescents aged 13–17 years in the US

summaries.title: 2022_PMC9707357_Sociodemographic_Variation_in_Early_Uptake_of_COVID-19_Vaccine_a
summaries.ehr_evidence: CDC analyzed data from 4,496 National Immunization Survey-Child COVID Module (NIS-CCM) interviews.
summaries.summary: Analysis of sociodemographic variations in COVID-19 vaccine uptake and parental attitudes among children aged 6 months–4 years in the United States.
key_findings: 3.5% of children aged 6 months–4 years were vaccinated.
59.3% were unvaccinated but parents were open to vaccination.
37.2% were unvaccinated and parents were reluctant to vaccinate.
Openness to vaccination was higher among parents of Hispanic, Black, and Asian children than among parents of White children.
Parental confidence and receipt of provider recommendations were lower among unvaccinated children.
summaries.population: Children aged 6 months–4 years, United States

summaries.title: Antecedents and correlates of improved cognitive performance in children exposed in utero to low levels of lead.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR used; the study is a prospective cohort study using clinical measurements and blood samples.
summaries.summary: A prospective study investigating the impact of prenatal lead exposure on cognitive development in children from 24 to 57 months of age, identifying factors that correlate with cognitive recovery.
key_findings: High prenatal lead exposure (10-254 µg/dL) is associated with lower cognitive scores at 2 years.
Children appear to recover or compensate for early lead insults by 57 months.
Recovery is associated with lower postnatal blood lead, higher socioeconomic status, higher HOME scores, higher maternal IQ, and female gender.
Risk of persistent cognitive deficit is higher in children with high prenatal exposure combined with high postnatal exposure or less optimal sociodemographic characteristics.
summaries.population: 170 children (median age 57.8 months)

summaries.title: Semiparametric modeling of age at achieving developmental milestones after prenatal exposure to methylmercury in the Seychelles child development study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a longitudinal cohort (SCDS) with maternal hair mercury measurements and parent interviews.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of the effects of prenatal methylmercury exposure on the age at which children achieved developmental milestones (walking and talking) using semiparametric generalized additive models in the Seychelles Child Development Study.
key_findings: Very slight delays (<1 day) in walking were seen at mercury levels 0-7 ppm, but did not persist at higher levels.
No evidence for any association between prenatal exposure and age at talking.
summaries.study_design: Longitudinal cohort study using semiparametric generalized additive models
summaries.population: 779 Seychelloian children
limitations: Difficult to conclude causal/effect relationship at low exposure levels

summaries.title: Air pollution and health effects: A study of medical visits among children in Santiago, Chile.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data on morbidity due to respiratory diseases among children under 15 year of age have been collected from a group of public primary health clinics... the total number of child medical visits and respiratory morbidity diagnoses was collected every day.
summaries.summary: A time-series analysis examining the association between air pollution (PM10 and ozone) and pediatric respiratory morbidity in Santiago, Chile.
key_findings: Significant association between PM10 and lower respiratory symptoms in children ages 3-15 and children under age 2.
PM10 is associated with medical visits related to upper respiratory symptoms in the older cohort.
Ozone is associated with visits related to both lower and upper respiratory symptoms in the older cohort.
A 50-pg/m3 change in PM10 is associated with a 4-12% increase in lower respiratory symptoms for children under age 2.
summaries.population: children 3-15 years of age and children under age 2 in Santiago, Chile
limitations: missing values or insufficient information in some clinics
exclusion of clinics based on distance from monitoring stations

summaries.title: Minority children at risk from ETS.

key_findings: Biomarkers (cotinine, carcinogenic protein complexes, sister chromatid exchanges) increased with ETS exposure in all groups.
summaries.population: 109 Hispanic and African-American children, ages 1 to 6

summaries.title: Evaluation of urinary porphyrin excretion in neonates born to mothers exposed to airborne hexachlorobenzene.

summaries.title: Metal composition of ambient PM2.5 influences severity of allergic airways disease in mice.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None

key_findings: Hypothesized that PM2.5 from Hettstedt would enhance allergic sensitization, airway responsiveness, and lung inflammation compared to PM2.5 from Zerbst.

summaries.title: Traffic-related air pollution and respiratory symptoms in children living along trunk roads in Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. Data were collected via school surveys and ambient air monitoring stations.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study investigating the association between traffic-related air pollution (NO2, SPM) and respiratory symptoms (asthma) in children living near trunk roads in Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
key_findings: Prevalence of asthma was higher in girls living < 50m from trunk roads.
Incidence of asthma in boys living in roadside areas significantly increased (OR = 3.75).
Prevalence of asthma in girls increased significantly with air pollution concentration.
summaries.population: 3,234 children (aged 6-9 years) in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
summaries.exposure_domain: traffic-related air pollution (NO2, SPM, automobile exhaust)

Quantified relative contribution of different lead sources (lower limit)

summaries.title: Blood lead changes during pregnancy and postpartum with calcium supplementation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Longitudinal study on lead mobilization from maternal skeleton during pregnancy and postpartum, evaluating the effect of calcium supplementation in immigrants to Australia.

Blood lead increased by 10-50% (geometric mean 25%) during the third trimester.
Increases generally occurred at 6-8 months gestation.

summaries.summary: The study investigated the effects of prenatal exposure to air pollutants (O3, CO, PM10, and NO2) on birth weight and intrauterine growth retardation (IUGR) in term newborns in southern California.

key_findings: Hypothesized that prenatal lead exposure would have a more powerful and lasting impact on child development than postnatal exposure.

Variable individual lead exposure reduced BPb tracking

summaries.title: Vaccine-associated paralytic poliomyelitis: a case report of flaccid monoparesis after oral polio vaccine.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The manuscript is a clinical case report.
summaries.summary: A case report describing the first officially approved case of vaccine-associated paralytic poliomyelitis (VAPP) in Korea, involving a 4-month-old male who developed flaccid paralysis after receiving the oral polio vaccine (OPV).
key_findings: First officially approved case of recipient VAPP in Korea.
Flaccid paralysis was detected 4 weeks after OPV immunization.
Symptoms included hypotonic monoparesis, hypotonia, and atrophy of the left lower extremity.
summaries.population: 4-month-old male patient in Korea
limitations: Not an epidemiological or exposome study

summaries.title: Protection of newborn animals through maternal immunization.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR data used; the study is a veterinary overview.
summaries.summary: The manuscript discusses the use of maternal vaccination to provide passive immunity to neonatal animals, highlighting differences in placental transport across species and providing examples in cattle, dogs, and pigs.
key_findings: Maternal vaccination is a safe and efficient method for providing passive immunity to neonates.
Placental structure determines the nature of molecular transport.
Vaccines for calf diarrhea are well-established and enhance antibody titers in colostrum and milk.

summaries.title: Increased reproductive success of women after prenatal undernutrition.
summaries.ehr_evidence: birth records have been kept at the Wilhelmina Gasthuis
summaries.summary: Study of the Dutch Famine Birth Cohort investigating whether prenatal exposure to famine (1944-1945) affected the reproductive success of offspring.

key_findings: PM2.5 increase (17.4 μg/m3) associated with 8.8% increase in wheezing

Mother tongue of parents is the most pronounced association with overweight and check-ups
Children from non-German-speaking families tend to be more overweight and have fewer check-ups
Regional level influences are small when individual level social variables are controlled

CAF01 reduced post-challenge bacterial growth of M. bovis BCG.

summaries.title: Satisfactory safety and immunogenicity of MSP3 malaria vaccine candidate in Tanzanian children aged 12-24 months.

Vaccination induced strong cytophilic IgG responses (IgG1 and IgG3) similar to those in African adults with premunition.

summaries.title: Temporal and spatial patterns of ambient endotoxin concentrations in Fresno, California.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used ambient monitoring sites, schools, and residences for air sample collection rather than electronic health records.

key_findings: Endotoxin concentration patterns were primarily determined by meteorologic factors, specifically air stagnation.
Highest concentrations were found in areas immediately downwind from agricultural/pasture land.
Coarse particulate matter (PMc) was the only pollutant correlated with endotoxin.
Endotoxin was the most spatially variable pollutant measured.

singleton status

maternal smoking

key_findings: Early-life C. leptum exposure increased regulatory T (Treg) cells in cesarean-delivered mice.
Mice exposed to C. leptum and ovalbumin (OVA) did not develop typical asthma phenotypes.
C. leptum exposure induced an immunosuppressive environment in the lung, inhibiting Th1, Th2, Th9, and Th17 cell responses.
captured_features: Th1, Th2, Th9, Th17 cell subsets
summaries.population: infant mice
summaries.data_availability_statement: All relevant data are within the paper.

summaries.title: Long-term effect of early-life stress from earthquake exposure on working memory in adulthood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR or linked health data mentioned; subjects were enrolled and tested using standardized cognitive assessments (BVMT-R and HVLT-R).
summaries.summary: A study investigating the long-term effects of early-life stress from the 1976 Tangshan earthquake on working memory performance in adulthood.
key_findings: Infant exposure to earthquake-related stress impairs visuospatial memory in adulthood.
Fetuses in the second and third trimesters are more vulnerable to stress-induced damage than those in the first trimester.
Education level and early-life earthquake exposure are independent risk factors for reduced visuospatial memory.
summaries.population: 907 subjects born and raised in Tangshan, China
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Effectiveness of introducing point of care capillary testing and linking screening with routine appointments for increasing blood lead screening rates of young children: a before-after study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Screening rates were determined from routinely collected service data
summaries.summary: A before-after study evaluating the effectiveness of introducing point-of-care capillary testing and linking blood lead screening with routine child health programs (like immunizations) to increase screening rates in children in Broken Hill, Australia.
key_findings: Annual screening rate increased from 0.39 in 2008 to 0.75 in 2012.
Greatest increases were observed in children aged 11–23 and 48–59 months.
Linking screening with well-accepted mainstream child health programs is an effective strategy to improve participation.
summaries.population: Pre-school aged children in Broken Hill, Australia
limitations: Geographically specific to a mining town

summaries.title: Exposure to grass pollen--but not birch pollen--affects lung function in Swedish children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Swedish birth cohort BAMSE clinical examination
summaries.summary: Study investigating the impact of pollen exposure (grass vs birch) and air pollutants on lung function in Swedish children.
key_findings: Grass pollen exposure the day before testing was associated with reduced FEV1 in 8-year-olds.
The negative effect was stronger in children sensitized to pollen allergens.
Birch pollen did not show a similar effect.
summaries.population: Swedish children (8 and 16 years old)

summaries.title: Effects of 4-nonylphenol on oxidant/antioxidant balance system inducing hepatic steatosis in male rat.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is an experimental animal model using Sprague-Dawley rats.
summaries.summary: Experimental study investigating the effects of 4-nonylphenol (4-NP) on hepatic steatosis, oxidative stress, and gene expression in male rats.
key_findings: 4-NP induces hepatic steatosis and apoptosis in rats.
Up-regulated genes involved in apoptosis, hepatotoxicity, and oxidative stress were observed.
Increased ROS and decreased antioxidant enzymes were found in 4-NP exposed rats.
Elevated serum GOT, GPT, LDH, and gamma-GT were observed.
summaries.population: 24 male Sprague-Dawley rats

summaries.title: Mitochondrial oxidative DNA damage and exposure to particulate air pollution in mother-newborn pairs.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the ENVIRONAGE birth cohort, which is a prospective birth cohort, not an EHR or linked health database.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the association between particulate matter (PM) exposure during pregnancy and mitochondrial oxidative DNA damage (8-OHdG) in mothers and newborns.
key_findings: PM10 exposure during entire pregnancy and last trimester associated with increased maternal 8-OHdG.
PM2.5 exposure during entire pregnancy and third trimester associated with increased maternal 8-OHdG.
PM10 exposure during first and second trimesters associated with increased cord blood 8-OHdG.
summaries.population: Mother-newborn pairs (singletons) in Genk, Belgium

summaries.title: Is There a Causal Relation between Maternal Acetaminophen Administration and ADHD?
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a murine model.
summaries.summary: An experimental study using a murine model to investigate whether prenatal acetaminophen (APAP) exposure causes hyperkinetic dysfunction (ADHD-like behavior) in offspring.
key_findings: No significant differences in locomotor activity, brain volumes, or neuronal quantifications were found in offspring exposed to APAP.
summaries.population: Pregnant CD1 mice and offspring
limitations: Lack of accurate assessment of ADHD in murine models
Results should be taken with caution when compared to clinical data
summaries.data_availability_statement: All relevant data are within the paper and its Supporting Information files.

summaries.title: Feasibility and acceptability of oral cholera vaccine mass vaccination campaign in response to an outbreak and floods in Malawi.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Documentation of the first mass oral cholera vaccine (OCV) campaign in Malawi in response to a 2014/15 outbreak and floods.
key_findings: 97.6% coverage in first round
85.8% coverage in second round
Feasible and acceptable for large-scale OCV campaign
summaries.population: 160,000+ people aged 1+ years in Nsanje district, Malawi

summaries.title: Prenatal Exposure to Phthalates and Anogenital Distance in Male Infants from a Low-Exposed Danish Cohort (2010-2012).
summaries.ehr_evidence: Odense Patient data Exploratory Network (OPEN) and Odense University Hospital
summaries.summary: Prospective study investigating the association between maternal urinary phthalate metabolites during pregnancy and anogenital distance (AGD) and penile width in male offspring in a Danish cohort.
key_findings: Not provided in the snippet
summaries.population: 273 mother-son pairs in the Odense Child Cohort study (Denmark)
limitations: Results not provided in the snippet

summaries.title: Severe and Moderate Asthma Exacerbations in Asthmatic Children and Exposure to Ambient Air Pollutants.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Exacerbations of asthma... were deﬁned as one hospitalization or emergency room visit with a diagnosis of asthma... from a population-based cohort of asthmatic children in the province of Quebec (Canada).
summaries.summary: The study assessed the association between birth and annual exposure to outdoor air pollutants (O3, NO2, and PM2.5) and severe asthma exacerbations in a population-based cohort of asthmatic children in Quebec, Canada.
key_findings: Time-dependent exposure to NO2, O3, and PM2.5 was significantly associated with asthma exacerbations.
No associations were found between exposure at birth to NO2 or O3 and asthma exacerbations.
summaries.population: asthmatic children born between 1 April 1996 and 31 March 2011 in Quebec, Canada
limitations: residential postal code estimation for pollutant levels

summaries.title: Bisphenol A and Adiposity in an Inner-City Birth Cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort (CCCEH) with direct urine samples and anthropometric measurements.
summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study investigating the association between prenatal and early-childhood bisphenol A (BPA) exposure and childhood adiposity measures (FMI, %BF, WC) in a New York City birth cohort.
key_findings: Prenatal urinary BPA concentrations were positively associated with child age 7 FMI, %BF, and WC.
Child urinary BPA concentrations were not associated with childhood anthropometric outcomes.
summaries.population: African American and Dominican women (ages 18-35) and their children in Northern Manhattan and the South Bronx, NYC
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary Materials(177 KB) PDF Click here for additional data file.

summaries.title: Moderate Prenatal Cadmium Exposure and Adverse Birth Outcomes: a Role for Sex-Specific Differences?
summaries.ehr_evidence: The sample was derived from the ALSPAC study, a population-based study investigating environmental and genetic influences on the health, behaviour, and development of children.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the association between moderate prenatal cadmium exposure and birth outcomes (birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length) in a large cohort of pregnant women, highlighting potential sex-specific differences.
key_findings: Maternal blood cadmium was adversely associated with birthweight and crown-heel length in the whole group.
Sex-specific analysis revealed adverse associations with birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length in girls, but not in boys.
summaries.population: Pregnant women in the ALSPAC cohort
limitations: Sex-specific effects may mask associations in mixed-sex studies

summaries.title: Effects of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child IQ at 4 and 8 years in a UK birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses biological samples (whole blood) and clinical assessments from the ALSPAC birth cohort, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal birth cohort study investigating the association between prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at ages 4 and 8 in a UK population.
key_findings: No association of prenatal lead exposure with child IQ at 4 or 8 years in adjusted regression models.
Positive association for IQ at age 8 in girls (verbal 0.71, performance 0.57, total 0.73).
Evidence suggesting boys may be more susceptible to prenatal lead exposure than girls.
summaries.population: Pregnant women and offspring in the ALSPAC cohort (UK)
limitations: Small sample size of offspring at 30 months (n=235)

summaries.title: Reduction of in utero lead exposures in South African populations: Positive impact of unleaded petrol.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. Data were collected from biological fluids (maternal blood, urine, and cord blood) measured using Inductive Coupled Plasma Mass spectrometry (ICP-MS).
summaries.summary: The study assessed in utero lead (Pb) exposure and birth outcomes in a South African population following the removal of lead from petrol.
key_findings: Positive correlation between maternal blood Pb and cord blood Pb.
Geographical differences in gestational age, birth length, head circumference, Apgar score, and parity.
Positive association between cord blood Pb and head circumference in female neonates.
Maternal Pb levels correlated with race, educational status, water sources, cooking fuels, and pesticide use.
summaries.population: Pregnant women and neonates in South Africa
limitations: Study does not evaluate effects in young children or adults
Geographical differences in birth outcomes may be influenced by multiple confounding factors

summaries.title: The Hokkaido Birth Cohort Study on Environment and Children's Health: cohort profile-updated 2017.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective birth cohort study using direct specimen collection (blood, hair, breast milk) and face-to-face tests, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A profile and update of the Hokkaido Study on Environment and Children’s Health, a prospective birth cohort study investigating the effects of low-level environmental chemical exposures on birth outcomes and child development.
key_findings: Demonstrated effects of environmental chemical exposures on genetically susceptible populations and DNA methylation.
Identified associations between chemical exposure and birth size, neonatal hormone levels, neurobehavioral development, asthma, allergies, and infectious diseases.
captured_features: Birth outcomes (miscarriage, stillbirth, low birth weight, preterm birth, small for gestational age)
summaries.population: Pregnant women and children in Sapporo and Hokkaido, Japan
summaries.exposure_domain: dioxins, PCBs, organochlorine pesticides, PFAS, phthalates, bisphenol A, methylmercury, tobacco smoking
limitations: Report is a cohort profile/update rather than a final comprehensive analysis

summaries.title: Body Burden of Dichlorodiphenyl Dichloroethene (DDE) and Childhood Pulmonary Function.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from an environmental epidemiologic study, not an EHR.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal study in central Germany investigating the relationship between blood DDE levels and pulmonary function (FVC and FEV1) in children aged 8-10 years, specifically assessing whether the association is mediated by height and weight.
key_findings: DDE measured at eight years had significant, inverse, indirect, and total effects on FVC at ten years and FEV1 at ten years, mediated through effects of DDE on height and weight.
summaries.population: Children aged 8-10 years in central Germany
limitations: Need for validation in regions with ongoing, high DDT application

summaries.title: Proteomic Biomarkers for Bisphenol A-Early Exposure and Women's Thyroid Cancer.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses laboratory mice and clinical samples from 28 women thyroid cancer patients, not EHR.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the effects of early-life Bisphenol A (BPA) exposure on thyroid proteins in mice and identifies ANXA6 and VCP as potential proteomic biomarkers in human thyroid cancer patients.
key_findings: 17 proteins were altered in a BPA dose-dependent manner in mouse thyroid tissues.
ANXA6 and VCP were identified as potential proteomic biomarkers.
Positive association between blood BPA levels and mRNA expression of ANXA6 and VCP in human thyroid cancer tissues.
captured_features: Proteomic profiles (2D gel electrophoresis, mass spectrometry)
summaries.study_design: Experimental animal study and clinical biomarker analysis
summaries.population: Female ICR mice and 28 women thyroid cancer patients
limitations: Reliance on animal models for initial protein identification

summaries.title: Assessment of Nutritional Status of Infants Living in Arsenic-Contaminated Areas in Bangladesh and Its Association with Arsenic Exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; data was collected via direct anthropometric measurements and water testing.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study of 120 infants in arsenic-contaminated areas of Bangladesh to assess the association between drinking water arsenic levels and nutritional status (stunting, wasting, underweight) over 9 months.
key_findings: Significant association between stunting at 9 months and household drinking water arsenic exposure ≥50 μg/L (p = 0.009).
No significant changes in other nutritional indices over time or with arsenic levels.
captured_features: Weight and height measurements (WAZ, HAZ, WHZ)
Tube well drinking water arsenic concentration
summaries.population: 120 infants (birth to 9 months) in rural Bangladesh

summaries.title: Correction to: Early-life exposure to PM2.5 and risk of acute asthma clinical encounters among children in Massachusetts: a case-crossover analysis.
summaries.ehr_evidence: acute asthma clinical encounters
summaries.summary: Correction to a study on early-life PM2.5 exposure and risk of acute asthma clinical encounters among children in Massachusetts.
key_findings: Correction of Maternal Language Preference count from 240 (0.7%) to 886 (2.7%)
summaries.population: children in Massachusetts

summaries.title: Low-fidelity Venezuelan equine encephalitis virus polymerase mutants to improve live-attenuated vaccine safety and efficacy.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Laboratory study investigating low-fidelity mutations in the RdRp of the Venezuelan equine encephalitis virus (VEEV) vaccine strain to evaluate effects on attenuation and immunogenicity.
key_findings: Mutants TC-83 3x and TC-83 4x showed increased immunogenicity and protection in mice.
Mutants showed increased virulence during serial passaging.
Low-fidelity mutations may increase immunogenicity but risk vaccine instability.
summaries.population: Murine models (infant and adult mice)

summaries.title: Local- and regional-scale air pollution modelling (PM(10)) and exposure assessment for pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood to age 15 years: Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents And Children (ALSPAC) birth cohort and residential address history; no EHR or linked health data are mentioned.
summaries.summary: Development and evaluation of PM10 air pollution modelling and exposure assessment for pregnancy trimesters, infancy, and childhood (up to age 15) using the ALSPAC birth cohort.
key_findings: PM10 exposures fell from 31.2 μg/m3 at age 1 to 19.6 μg/m3 at age 15.
Local sources contributed 18.5%–19.5% during pregnancy and infancy.
Main roads contributed ~3.0% to total PM10 exposures in all periods.
summaries.study_design: Prospective birth cohort study with dispersion modelling
summaries.population: Children and mothers in the ALSPAC birth cohort (South West England)
limitations: Lack of fixed-site PM10 monitoring sites during the study period
Reliance on dispersion modelling for historic sub-annual periods
summaries.data_availability_statement: Supplementary Materials

summaries.title: Parents' attitudes towards topical fluoride and vaccines for children: Are these distinct or overlapping phenomena?
summaries.ehr_evidence: dental chart-based topical fluoride refusal
summaries.summary: A study examining the overlap between parental attitudes and behaviors regarding topical fluoride treatment and child vaccines to determine if these behaviors are distinct or correlated.
key_findings: Concordance between fluoride and vaccine refusal behaviors
Vaccine safety concerns, perceived severity of cavities, and internet use for vaccine information were significant for vaccine refusal in adjusted models
None of the fluoride-specific items were significantly associated with fluoride refusal in adjusted models
summaries.data_source_type: Clinical records (dental charts) and survey data
summaries.population: 361 children from three Washington state dental clinics

summaries.title: Total mercury exposure in early pregnancy has no adverse association with scholastic ability of the offspring particularly if the mother eats fish.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC), which is a birth cohort study.
summaries.summary: Analysis of prenatal mercury exposure and its impact on the scholastic abilities (reading, spelling, math, science) of children in the ALSPAC cohort.
key_findings: No adverse association between prenatal mercury and scholastic abilities, provided the mother ate fish.
Math and science skills were unaffected by maternal mercury levels when fish was consumed.
captured_features: scholastic test results (reading, spelling, phoneme awareness, mathematics, science)
summaries.population: children in the UK (ALSPAC cohort)
limitations: varying number of participants with complete data (1500 to 2200)

summaries.title: Strategic Response to an Outbreak of Circulating Vaccine-Derived Poliovirus Type 2 - Syria, 2017-2018.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; data were collected via public health surveillance, stool specimen collection, and vaccination records.
summaries.summary: Public health surveillance and response to an outbreak of circulating vaccine-derived poliovirus type 2 (cVDPV2) in Syria between 2017 and 2018.
key_findings: 74 cVDPV2 cases identified
41% of cases occurred in children who had never received a dose of OPV
mOPV2 vaccination campaigns successfully interrupted transmission
summaries.population: Children in Syria (Deir-ez-Zor governorate)
limitations: Impact of civil war on healthcare system and surveillance quality

summaries.title: Occupational exposure to organic solvents during pregnancy and childhood behavior: findings from the PELAGIE birth cohort (France, 2002-2013).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort (PELAGIE) with self-reported exposure and questionnaire-based behavioral assessments.
summaries.summary: A prospective birth cohort study investigating the longitudinal association between prenatal occupational exposure to organic solvents by mothers and the behavioral traits of their children at ages 2 and 6.
key_findings: Increased externalizing behavior at age 2 associated with prenatal solvent exposure.
Association attenuated by age 6.
No association observed for internalizing behavior.
summaries.population: Children of pregnant women in the PELAGIE cohort (France)

summaries.title: Analysis By Deep Sequencing of Discontinued Neurotropic Yellow Fever Vaccine Strains.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses archive vaccine strains and deep sequencing.
summaries.summary: Genomic analysis of the discontinued French neurotropic yellow fever (FNV) vaccine strain to investigate its genetic instability and neurotropic properties.
key_findings: FNV is genetically unstable.
Adaptation of FNV contributed to the neurotropic adverse phenotype.
FNV showed lower polymorphism content than the wild-type parental strain.
summaries.study_design: Genomic analysis of archive vaccine strains
summaries.population: Discontinued vaccine strains

summaries.title: A rapid qualitative assessment of oral cholera vaccine anticipated acceptability in a context of resistance towards cholera intervention in Nampula, Mozambique.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study used questionnaires, in-depth interviews, focus group discussions, and observations.
summaries.summary: A qualitative assessment of the anticipated acceptability of an oral cholera vaccine (OCV) campaign in Nampula, Mozambique, identifying barriers and levers in a context of political resistance.
key_findings: Politically motivated resistance to cholera interventions exists due to past violence.
Vaccine hesitancy is linked to past school-based vaccine issues and safety rumors.
Perceived vulnerability to cholera may override vaccine hesitancy.
Successful social mobilization requires involvement of community leaders and multi-party representation.
summaries.population: Government informants, community leaders, and community members in Nampula, Mozambique

summaries.title: Sex-Dependent Impact of Low-Level Lead Exposure during Prenatal Period on Child Psychomotor Functions.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the Polish Mother and Child Cohort (REPRO_PL), which collected questionnaire data and biological samples.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child psychomotor development (up to 24 months) in a Polish cohort, focusing on sex-dependent susceptibility.
key_findings: No significant impact on girls' psychomotor abilities.
Boys showed lower cognitive scores associated with increasing cord blood lead levels (β = -2.07; p = 0.04).
No significant impact on language or motor abilities.
captured_features: Bayley Scales of Infant and Toddler Development scores
summaries.population: 402 children from the Polish Mother and Child Cohort (REPRO_PL)
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated Data Supplementary Materials Click here for additional data file.

summaries.title: Is intrauterine exposure to acetaminophen associated with emotional and hyperactivity problems during childhood? Findings from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort and the Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire (SDQ).
summaries.summary: A prospective longitudinal study investigating the association between intrauterine acetaminophen exposure and emotional and hyperactivity problems in children at ages 6 and 11 in a Brazilian birth cohort.
key_findings: Acetaminophen was used by 27.5% of mothers during pregnancy.
Intrauterine exposure increased the odds of emotional problems (OR 1.47) and hyperactivity/inattention (OR 1.42) in 6-year-old boys.
No association was observed for girls at either age.
The effect size for boys decreased slightly at age 11.
summaries.population: Children in the 2004 Pelotas birth cohort (Brazil)

summaries.title: Quantitative characterization of the urine and serum metabolomes of children is essential for 'omics' studies.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study was part of HELIX, a collaborative project between six longitudinal birth cohort studies.
summaries.summary: Commentary on a study by Lau et al. characterizing serum and urine metabolomes of European children to establish a reference for future exposome studies.
key_findings: Metabolites were associated with age, sex, body mass index (BMI), and dietary intake.
Novel association between urinary 4-deoxyerythronic acid and body mass index was uncovered.
summaries.population: European children
summaries.exposure_domain: Early-life physical and chemical exposures (e.g., pollution, diet)
limitations: Lack of existing metabolomic reference profiles for children
summaries.data_availability_statement: Not applicable.

summaries.title: PMC2 title
summaries.ehr_evidence: n/a
summaries.summary: s

summaries.title: Prenatal Exposure to Select Phthalates and Phenols and Associations with Fetal and Placental Weight among Male Births in the EDEN Cohort (France).
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective cohort (EDEN) with urine samples, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: Investigation of maternal prenatal exposure to phthalates and phenols and its association with fetal and placental weight in a French cohort.
key_findings: Triclosan and MCNP negatively associated with placental weight
Benzophenone-3 and sum of parabens positively associated with placental weight
MCNP and MCOP negatively associated with PFR
summaries.population: 473 mother-son pairs in the EDEN cohort (France)

summaries.title: Development of a novel multiplex electrochemiluminescent-based immunoassay to aid enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli vaccine development and evaluations.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Development and validation of a multiplex electrochemiluminescent (ECL) immunoassay using the Meso Scale Discovery (MSD) platform to analyze immune responses to Enterotoxigenic Escherichia coli (ETEC) antigens.
key_findings: The MSD platform achieved excellent correlations with ELISA for the antigens tested.
A single dilution of sample provides a quantifiable result for a wide range of sample titers.
captured_features: Antibody levels for ETEC antigens (CFA/I, CS1, CS2, CS3, CS5, CS6, LTA, LTB)
summaries.population: Not specified (laboratory samples)

summaries.title: Dietary Patterns Are Not Consistently Associated with Variability in Blood Lead Concentrations in Pregnant British Women.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were derived from the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC) birth cohort, which used blood samples and food-frequency questionnaires.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of dietary patterns and food groups associated with blood lead concentrations (B-Pb) in pregnant women.
key_findings: Negative association between 'confectionery' pattern and B-Pb ≥5 µg/dL
Positive association between 'all leafy green and green vegetables' and B-Pb ≥5 µg/dL
Negative association between 'cakes and biscuits' and B-Pb ≥5 µg/dL
Positive association of 'healthy' diet pattern after multiple imputation
summaries.population: Pregnant women in the UK (ALSPAC cohort)
summaries.exposure_domain: Dietary patterns, food groups, blood lead
limitations: Limited evidence of association between typical diet and B-Pb
Lack of extensive food composition data
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary Materialsnxz023_Supplemental_FileClick here for additional data file.

summaries.title: Mitotically heritable effects of BMAA on striatal neural stem cell proliferation and differentiation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: An in vitro study investigating the effects of the cyanobacterial toxin BMAA on the proliferation, differentiation, and mitotic heritability of neural stem cells (NSCs).
key_findings: BMAA exposure at 250 µM reduced NSC proliferation through apoptosis and G2/M arrest.
BMAA exposure at 50–100 µM reduced the differentiation of NSCs into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
BMAA-induced changes in neurons derived from treated NSCs were mitotically heritable to daughter cells.
BMAA-induced morphological alterations included reduced neurite length and decreased number of processes.
captured_features: differentiation into astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons
summaries.population: Primary striatal neurons and embryonic neural stem cells

summaries.title: Identification of the determinants of incomplete vaccination in Australian children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: child’s demographic and relevant clinical history was obtained from paper and electronic health records
summaries.summary: A study identifying sociodemographic and belief risk factors associated with incomplete vaccination in children under 6 years old in Perth, Australia.
key_findings: Children with incomplete vaccination were more likely to be born overseas
Larger family size was associated with incomplete vaccination
Parents of children with incomplete vaccination had lower educational attainment
Parents of children with incomplete status reported more doubt about vaccination importance and greater concern about safety
summaries.population: parents of children <6 years old in Perth, Western Australia
limitations: limited to a specific geographic location (Perth, WA)

summaries.title: The effect of H1N1 vaccination on serum miRNA expression in children: A tale of caution for microRNA microarray studies.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used serum samples obtained from an open-label randomised control trial, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: The study investigated changes in serum microRNA (miRNA) expression in children 21 days after receiving H1N1 vaccination using microarray profiling and RT-qPCR validation.
key_findings: 19 microRNAs were differentially expressed in the discovery cohort after vaccination.
Many microarray-detected microRNAs could not be validated by RT-qPCR.
RT-qPCR confirmed downregulation of miR-142-3p in the discovery cohort.
Findings were not replicated in the subsequent validation cohort.
summaries.population: Children aged 6 months to 12 years
limitations: Small sample size in validation cohort
Microarray results not consistently validated by RT-qPCR

summaries.title: The maternal antibody against diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis showed distinct regional difference in China.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used ELISA kits to measure antibodies in neonates hospitalized in two hospitals, rather than extracting data from an EHR database.
summaries.summary: A study comparing maternal antibody levels against diphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis in neonates from different economic regions in China.
key_findings: Neonates from Qianjiang had significantly lower anti-Dtx and anti-Ttx IgG levels than those from Shunyi.
Neonates from Qianjiang had a significantly higher detectable rate of anti-Ptx IgG.
summaries.population: 438 neonates from Beijing and southwestern China

summaries.title: Repeated holdout validation for weighted quantile sum regression.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data from the Swedish Environmental Longitudinal, Mother and Child, Asthma and Allergy (SELMA) study.
summaries.summary: The study proposes and validates a 'repeated holdout validation' method for Weighted Quantile Sum (WQS) regression to assess chemical mixtures in small environmental epidemiology samples, using prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals and child IQ.
key_findings: Repeated holdout validation improves the stability of WQS estimates in finite study samples.
WQS index estimate was significant without partitioning but attenuated and became nonsignificant using repeated holdout validation.
Repeated holdout can characterize uncertainty in identifying chemicals of concern.
captured_features: prenatal exposure to 26 endocrine disrupting chemicals
summaries.study_design: Methodological validation using a cohort study
summaries.population: 718 mother-child pairs

summaries.summary: s
