summaries.title: Kansan Guardian Perceptions of HPV and the HPV Vaccine and the Role of Social Media.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were collected via an online survey.
summaries.summary: Study evaluating Kansas guardians' perceptions of HPV and the HPV vaccine and the influence of social media messages on those perceptions.
key_findings: 57.9% of guardians saw HPV vaccine info on social media.
Guardians who saw social media info were more likely to perceive the vaccine as lethal.
Children of wealthier guardians were vaccinated more.
summaries.population: Kansas guardians of children aged 9-17 years

summaries.title: Serious adverse event: late neurotropic disease associated with yellow fever vaccine.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Case report of a healthy infant who developed late neurotropic disease following yellow fever vaccination.
key_findings: Infant presented with fever, chills, and neurological symptoms (strabismus, cervical muscle weakness) 20 days after yellow fever vaccination.
Diagnosis confirmed by positive IgM in liquor.
summaries.population: One infant
limitations: Not an exposome or EWAS study

summaries.title: Early-life inhalant allergen exposure, filaggrin genotype, and the development of sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a population-based birth cohort and home dust samples, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A longitudinal birth cohort study investigating how filaggrin (FLG) mutations modify the relationship between early-life inhalant allergen exposure (mite, cat, dog) and the development of allergic sensitization from infancy to adolescence.
key_findings: Significant interaction between FLG mutations and Fel d 1 exposure on cat sensitization, with higher risk in children with mutations.
Mite sensitization risk with Der p 1 exposure was consistently greater in children with FLG mutations.
Significant interaction between FLG mutations and dog ownership, where children with mutations exposed to dogs in infancy had significantly lower risk of sensitization.
captured_features: Mite, cat, and dog allergen levels in home dust
summaries.population: 1184 subjects in a population-based birth cohort (infancy to age 16 years)
summaries.exposure_domain: Inhalant allergens (house dust mite, cat, dog)
limitations: Not specified in the provided text
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary MaterialsOnline Repository

summaries.title: BCG Moreau Vaccine Safety Profile and NK Cells-Double Protection Against Disseminated BCG Infection in Retrospective Study of BCG Vaccination in 52 Polish Children with Severe Combined Immunodeficiency.
summaries.ehr_evidence: hospitalized in Children’s Memorial Health Institute, Warsaw (CMHI), in the years 1980–2015
summaries.summary: Retrospective study evaluating the safety profile of the BCG Moreau vaccine in 52 Polish children with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) compared to an international cohort.
key_findings: Significantly lower rate of disseminated BCG infections (19% vs 34% in Marciano study)
NK cells play a role in protecting SCID patients against disseminated BCG complications
HSCT and TB therapy are relevant for patient survival
summaries.population: 52 Polish children with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID)

summaries.title: Childhood type 1 diabetes: an environment-wide association study across England.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We obtained type 1 diabetes data from National Health Service (NHS) Digital hospital episode statistics (HES) records
summaries.summary: An ecological environment-wide association study (EnW AS) assessing associations between various environmental, demographic, and meteorological factors and childhood type 1 diabetes incidence in England.
key_findings: 15 out of 53 demographic and environmental risk factors were significantly associated with diabetes incidence
Air pollutants (PM10, NO2, NOx, CO) were inversely associated with incidence
Significant associations found with lead in soil, radon, outdoor light at night, overcrowding, population density, and ethnicity
Association found between type 1 diabetes and Index of Multiple Deprivation (IMD) and radon potential class
summaries.population: children aged 0–9 years in England
summaries.exposure_domain: air pollution, soil lead, radon, outdoor light at night, demographics, meteorological factors
summaries.data_availability_statement: The majority of the demographic and environmental factors included in the study were derived from Crown copyright data and are available via the links provided in the text and references. HES data are available via NHS Digital on application with appropriate ethics and governance permissions; we do not hold data provider, ethics or governance permissions to share these health data with third parties.

summaries.title: Unilateral morning glory disc anomaly in a patient with prenatal Zika virus exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is a single case report.
summaries.summary: A case report describing a 3-year-old male child with morning glory disc anomaly (MGDA) and other neurological defects following prenatal Zika virus (ZIKV) exposure.
key_findings: First report suggesting an association between prenatal ZIKV exposure and MGDA.
Prenatal ZIKV exposure may be associated with a wider pathologic spectrum than previously reported.
captured_features: Neuroimaging (base of skull encephalocele, callosal dysgenesis)
summaries.population: 3-year-old male child
summaries.data_availability_statement: All data generated or analyzed during this study are included in this manuscript.

summaries.title: Early-Life Famine Exposure and Risk of Cardiovascular Diseases in Later Life: Findings From the REACTION Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the REACTION (Risk Evaluation of Cancers in Chinese Diabetic Individuals: A Longitudinal) Study, which recruited community-dwelling adults.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between early-life exposure to the Chinese famine (1959-1962) and the risk of cardiovascular diseases (CVD) in adulthood.
key_findings: Early-life famine exposure (fetal, childhood, and adolescent) significantly increased the risk of total CVD, myocardial infarction, stroke, and coronary heart disease.
Individuals in severely affected famine areas had a significantly increased risk of total CVD in all three exposed groups.
summaries.population: 259,657 community-dwelling adults aged 40 years or older from mainland China

summaries.title: Long-Term Effects of Environmental Lead on Erythropoietin Production in Young Adults: A Follow-Up Study of a Prospective Cohort in Kosovo.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a prospective birth cohort and a biorepository for blood samples, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A follow-up study of a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo to evaluate the long-term effects of prenatal, early childhood, and concurrent lead (Pb) exposure on erythropoietin (EPO) production in young adulthood.
key_findings: Participants exposed to high Pb levels required increased EPO production to maintain normal hemoglobin (Hgb) at ages 4.5 and 6.5.
By ages 9.5 and 12, participants were no longer capable of hyper-production of EPO, suggesting cumulative toxicity to renal peritubular cells.
Reduction in Pb exposure may allow for a reversal of the impact on EPO production.
summaries.population: 101 participants (mean age 24.9 years) from a prospective birth cohort in Kosovo
limitations: Challenges in recruiting participants from Prishtina due to city expansion and demographic changes.
summaries.data_availability_statement: All pertinent data are in our study files and can be made available upon request.

summaries.title: Warp Speed for Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) Vaccines: Why Are Children Stuck in Neutral?
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: An opinion piece advocating for the immediate initiation of phase 2 clinical trials for COVID-19 vaccines in children to address health impacts, racial disparities, and educational/economic consequences.
key_findings: Advocates for parallel pediatric vaccine trials alongside adult efficacy trials.
Highlights high hospitalization rates and racial disparities in children with COVID-19.
Emphasizes the need for vaccines to mitigate the impact on children's education and well-being.
summaries.population: Children in the United States

summaries.title: DNA methylome signatures of prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids in hippocampus and peripheral whole blood of female guinea pigs in early life.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses a guinea pig animal model.
summaries.summary: The study examines DNA methylation signatures in the hippocampus and whole blood of guinea pig offspring following prenatal exposure to synthetic glucocorticoids (sGC) to assess epigenetic biomarkers of common molecular pathways.
key_findings: Identified tissue-specific and common methylation signatures of prenatal sGC exposure.
134 genes were modified in both hippocampus and blood, with 61 showing changes at identical CpG sites.
Prenatal sGC exposure alters the methylation status of gene clusters involved in brain development.
summaries.population: Female guinea pig offspring
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data discussed in this publication have been deposited in NCBI ’s Gene Expression Omnibus 48 and are accessible through GEO Series accession number GSE163447.

summaries.title: Assessment of chronic bronchitis and risk factors in young adults: results from BAMSE.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used questionnaire data and clinical measures from the Swedish BAMSE birth cohort, not electronic health records.
summaries.summary: Assessment of the prevalence and early-life risk factors (e.g., air pollution, breastfeeding) for chronic bronchitis in young adults using the Swedish BAMSE birth cohort.
key_findings: Prevalence of chronic bronchitis in young adults was 5.5%.
Daily smoking (aOR 3.85) and air pollution (black carbon at ages 1-4y, aOR 1.71) were associated with chronic bronchitis.
Exclusive breastfeeding for $\ge$4 months was associated with a lower risk of chronic bronchitis (aOR 0.66).
summaries.population: Young adults in a Swedish population-based birth cohort
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Aspartame and cancer - new evidence for causation.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is a toxicological analysis of rodent models.
summaries.summary: A commentary and re-evaluation of Ramazzini Institute studies confirming that aspartame is a chemical carcinogen in rodents, with significant risks from prenatal exposure.
key_findings: Aspartame causes dose-related increases in malignant tumors in rodents.
Prenatal exposure to aspartame increases cancer risk in rodent offspring.
Immunohistochemical and morphological re-evaluation confirmed malignancy in 92.3% of cases.
summaries.population: Sprague-Dawley rats and Swiss mice

summaries.title: Immunogenicity and Safety of AS03-adjuvanted H5N1 Influenza Vaccine in Children 6-35 Months of Age: Results From a Phase 2, Randomized, Observer-blind, Multicenter, Dose-ranging Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a clinical trial, not an EHR-based study.
summaries.summary: A phase 2, randomized, observer-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging study evaluating the immunogenicity and safety of different formulations of an AS03-adjuvanted H5N1 influenza vaccine in children 6–35 months of age.
key_findings: All formulations were highly immunogenic.
1.9 µg HA/AS03B provided the most favorable balance of immunogenicity versus reactogenicity.
Antibody levels persisted >4 times above baseline 12 months after primary vaccination.
summaries.study_design: Phase 2, randomized, observer-blind, multicenter, dose-ranging study
summaries.population: children 6–35 months of age

summaries.title: Maternal high-fat diet modifies myelin organization, microglial interactions, and results in social memory and sensorimotor gating deficits in adolescent mouse offspring.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study uses a mouse model.
summaries.summary: Investigation of how maternal high-fat diet (mHFD) affects myelin organization, microglial interactions, and behavioral outcomes in adolescent mouse offspring.
key_findings: mHFD results in neuroinflammation and hypomyelination
increased myelin density with reduced cytosolic myelin channels in the corpus callosum of male offspring
lower transcripts of myelination-associated genes (e.g., Igf1) in the hippocampus of male offspring
microglia in the corpus callosum showed reduced mature lysosomes and increased synaptic contacts
mHFD-exposed offspring exhibited deficits in social memory and sensorimotor gating
summaries.population: Adolescent mouse offspring

summaries.title: The Rotavirus Vaccine Story: From Discovery to the Eventual Control of Rotavirus Disease.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A historical overview of the discovery of rotavirus, the development of various vaccines (RotaShield, Rotarix, Rotateq, Rotavac, Rotasiil), and the subsequent reduction in childhood diarrhea and mortality worldwide.
key_findings: Rotavirus is a leading pathogen causing severe diarrhea and a major cause of mortality in children under 5 years.
Vaccines have contributed to marked decreases in hospitalizations and deaths from diarrhea.
Live oral vaccines are less effective in low-income countries for reasons not yet understood.
summaries.population: Children under 5 years old (global)

summaries.title: Nasal spray live attenuated influenza vaccine: the first experience in Italy in children and adolescents during the 2020-21 season.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were collected via questionnaires given on vaccination day and follow-up contacts with parents 1-3 months later.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of the first experience of quadrivalent live attenuated influenza vaccine (qLAIV) in Italian children and adolescents during the 2020-21 season, focusing on adverse reactions, satisfaction, and information sources.
key_findings: Adverse events were reported in 24.8% of children (80.6% mild, 18.1% moderate, 1.3% significant).
Most common adverse events were rhinitis (52.5%) and fever (24.4%).
Children aged 2-5 years have an increased risk of adverse events compared to older groups.
High satisfaction reported by both parents (83.3%) and healthcare operators (93%).
captured_features: Number and severity of adverse reactions
summaries.population: Children aged 2-17 years in Italy

summaries.title: The association between in utero exposure to maternal psychological stress and female reproductive function in adolescence: A prospective cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a population-based pregnancy cohort study (The Raine Study) using prospective reporting and clinical examinations, not EHR.
summaries.summary: A prospective cohort study investigating the association between maternal psychological stress during pregnancy and reproductive function in female offspring during adolescence.
key_findings: Maternal stress in late gestation was associated with greater uterine volume and higher ovarian antral follicle count (AFC) in adolescents.
No associations were found between maternal stress and circulating AMH or Inhibin B.
captured_features: maternal stressful life events (early and late gestation)
summaries.population: 228 female offspring (age 14-16 years)

summaries.title: Early life exposure to house dust mite allergen prevents experimental allergic asthma requiring mitochondrial H(2)O(2).
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is an experimental animal model using mice.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the role of mitochondrial H2O2 in establishing airway tolerance to house dust mite (HDM) allergens in mice, demonstrating that early-life low-dose exposure prevents later allergic airway inflammation.
key_findings: Early-life exposure to low-dose HDM prevents allergic airway inflammation in later life.
Depletion of mitochondrial H2O2 in MCAT mice abrogated HDM-induced airway tolerance.
Low-dose HDM exposure constrains lung dendritic cell numbers to establish tolerance.
summaries.population: Newborn and young mice
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated DataSupplementary Materials1

summaries.title: Characterisation of lung function trajectories and associated early-life predictors in an Australian birth cohort study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The Raine Study is a large prospective cohort of 2900 pregnant women recruited from King Edward Memorial Hospital... Clinical and demographic data were collected during pregnancy and offspring assessed before birth and at 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 10, 14, 17, 18, 20, 22, 27 and 28 years of age.
summaries.summary: Characterization of lung function trajectories (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC) from 6 to 22 years and identification of early-life predictors in an Australian birth cohort.
key_findings: Four lung function trajectories identified
Asthma and maternal smoking identified as risk factors for low lung function trajectories
Early-life exposure to PM2.5 associated with low lung function trajectories
summaries.population: 1512 participants (children/young adults) from the Raine Study, Australia
summaries.data_availability_statement: Associated Data Supplementary Materials Please note: supplementary material is not edited by the Editorial Office, and is uploaded as it has been supplied by the author. Supplementary material 00072-2022.SUPPLEMENT Supplementary material 00072-2022.SUPPLEMENTARY_CLEAN_FS

summaries.title: Vaccine-Associated Measles Encephalitis in Immunocompromised Child, California, USA.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is a clinical case report.
summaries.summary: A case report of a fatal vaccine-associated measles encephalitis in an immunocompromised child in California, USA.
key_findings: Fatal measles encephalitis following MMR ProQuad vaccination in an immunocompromised child.
Whole-genome RNA sequencing confirmed measles virus infection with biased matrix-gene hypermutation.
summaries.population: One immunocompromised infant

summaries.title: Safety profile comparison of chimeric live attenuated and Vero cell-derived inactivated Japanese encephalitis vaccines through an active surveillance system in Australia.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were retrieved from SmartVax, an active surveillance system for monitoring vaccine safety utilized by more than 450 clinics and other immunization providers.
summaries.summary: Comparison of safety profiles (AEFIs) between two Japanese encephalitis (JE) vaccines (Imojev and JEspect) in Australia using active surveillance data.
key_findings: No significant differences in odds of overall, systemic, or local AEFIs between Imojev and JEspect.
Increased odds of overall AEFI in patients aged <5 years compared to those aged >50 years.
Both JE vaccines are safe and well tolerated.
summaries.population: 2756 patients in Australia
limitations: limited information on post-marketing safety of JE vaccines

summaries.title: COVID-19 vaccination in 5-11 years old children: Drivers of vaccine hesitancy among parents in Quebec.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Qualitative study exploring drivers of vaccine hesitancy among parents of children aged 5-11 in Quebec regarding the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine.
key_findings: Parents perceived children as at low risk for COVID-19 complications.
Hesitancy was driven by concerns over vaccine side effects (e.g., myocarditis) and perception of vaccine as unnecessary.
Some parents were motivated by a desire to return to normality and reduce transmission.
captured_features: perceived risk of vaccine side effects
summaries.population: Parents of children aged 5-11 years in Quebec, Canada

summaries.title: Prenatal exposure to insecticides and child cardiometabolic risk factors in the VHEMBE birth cohort.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses a birth cohort (VHEMBE) and peripartum biological samples, not EHR.
summaries.summary: Study investigating the impact of prenatal exposure to DDT and pyrethroid insecticides on cardiometabolic health markers in children at 5 years of age in South Africa.
key_findings: Maternal pyrethroid metabolites were associated with lower adiposity (reduced BMI z-scores, smaller waist circumferences, and decreased body fat percentages).
Maternal p,p’-DDT concentrations were associated with a reduction in body fat percentage.
summaries.population: Children in Limpopo, South Africa

summaries.title: COVID-19 mRNA Vaccine-Associated Myocarditis Presenting as STEMI in a 48-Year-Old Male.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; this is a clinical case report.
summaries.summary: Case report of a 48-year-old male who developed myocarditis presenting as STEMI shortly after receiving the second dose of the Moderna COVID-19 mRNA vaccine.
key_findings: Patient presented with chest pain and STEMI-like EKG findings after Moderna vaccine.
Symptoms and cardiac markers resolved with medical therapy.
Patient had a history of viral myocarditis 7 years prior.
summaries.population: 48-year-old male

summaries.title: Early-life exposure to hardship increased risk tolerance and entrepreneurship in adulthood with gender differences.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study uses geographical differences in famine intensity and weather shocks.
summaries.summary: The study investigates how early-life hardship during China's Great Famine (1959-1961) affected adult risk tolerance and entrepreneurship, specifically looking at gender differences and the mechanism of conditioning.
key_findings: Hardship during formative years increased risk tolerance in both men and women.
Hardship increased business ownership only among men.
The increase in entrepreneurship was at least partly due to conditioning rather than selection.
summaries.population: Chinese population (Great Famine cohort)
limitations: Specific to the historical context of China's Great Famine
summaries.data_availability_statement: Data Availability. China Population Census 1990, China Population Census 2000, China Population Mini-Census 2005, and China Economic Census data are available from the National Bureau of Statistics-Peking University (NBS-PKU) Research Data Center, Peking University (https://microdata.stats.gov.cn/; click on the third link). CHFSs 2011 and 2013 data are available from the Survey and Research Center for China Household Finance, Southwestern University of Finance and Economics, Chengdu ( https://chfs.swufe.edu.cn/; to access the data, apply through the official platform ( https://chfser.swufe.edu.cn/datas/). China General Social Surveys 2008, 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013 data are available from the National Survey Research Center, Renmin University of China, Beijing (http://cgss.ruc.edu.cn/English/Contact_Us.htm; to access the data, contact cgss@ruc.edu.cn). China Family Panel Studies 2010 data are available from the Institute of Social Science Survey, Peking University (http://www.isss.pku.edu.cn/cfps/; to access the restricted county-level data, apply through http://www.isss.pku.edu.cn/cfps/sjzx/xzsj/index.htm). China daily instrumental temperature records at 727 stations from 1951 to 1970 are available in Zenodo (https://zenodo.org/record/6084180#.YgsDzt9BxEY).

summaries.title: Willingness toward COVID-19 vaccination, coadministration with other vaccines and receive a COVID-19 vaccine booster: a cross-sectional study on the guardians of children in China.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used a self-administered questionnaire distributed through the 'Xiao Dou Miao' app.
summaries.summary: A cross-sectional study investigating the willingness of guardians in China to vaccinate their children against COVID-19, including coadministration with other vaccines and booster doses.
key_findings: 86.68% of guardians were willing to vaccinate their children with the COVID-19 vaccine.
77% of guardians were willing toward the coadministration of COVID-19 and influenza vaccines.
82% of guardians were willing to vaccinate their children with a COVID-19 vaccine booster.
captured_features: Guardian demographics (age, education, occupation, annual income)
Willingness scores for vaccination, coadministration, and boosters
summaries.population: Guardians of children in China

summaries.title: Active surveillance of adverse events after immunization (AEFI) from the Local Health Unitof Ferrara, Italy.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data were collected via mobile phone-SMS from parents.
summaries.summary: Active surveillance of adverse events following immunization (AEFI) in children under 24 months in Ferrara, Italy, using an SMS reporting tool.
key_findings: 983 SMS messages obtained from parents
1,984 vaccine doses recorded
99.5% of AEFI were classified as 'not serious'
Most reports related to General Disorders and Administration Site Conditions (52.3%)
pathologies_diseases: General Disorders and Administration Site Conditions
summaries.population: Parents of children who received vaccinations in the first 24 months of life in Ferrara, Italy
limitations: SMS reporting relies on parent awareness and participation

summaries.title: Repeatable enhancement of healthcare data with social determinants of health.
summaries.ehr_evidence: linking compiled consumer data to patient records in Electronic Health Records
summaries.summary: Development of a repeatable process to enrich Electronic Health Records (EHR) with social determinants of health (SDOH) data from commercial consumer sources.
key_findings: SDOH factors like home-ownership, marital-status, presence of children, number of members per household, economic stability, and education were significantly different between COVID-19 positive and negative groups.
Estimated family-income and home market-value were not significantly different.
summaries.population: patients (COVID-19 positive and negative groups)
limitations: consumer data coverage ranged from 90% to 54%
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets presented in this article are not readily available because it contains clinical information. Requests to access the datasets should be directed to mlgreer@uams.edu.

summaries.title: Updated report of COVID-19 vaccine safety monitoring in Japan: Booster shots and paediatric vaccinations.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Reports from healthcare professionals and the marketing authorisation holder (MAH).
summaries.summary: Safety monitoring of COVID-19 mRNA vaccines in Japan, focusing on booster shots and pediatric vaccinations.
key_findings: Reporting rates of myocarditis/pericarditis were generally lower for the third shot than for the first and second shots.
No cases were confirmed by experts suggesting a causal relationship between vaccination and death.
summaries.population: Japanese population (including children 5-11 years)

summaries.title: Maternal use of methamphetamine induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Investigation into how prenatal methamphetamine exposure affects myocardial gene expression in adult offspring, showing sex-dependent changes.
key_findings: Prenatal methamphetamine exposure induces sex-dependent changes in myocardial gene expression in adult offspring.
Female offspring exhibited 184 gene expression changes compared to 74 in males.
Dimethylarginine dimethylaminohydrolase 2 and 3-hydroxybutyrate dehydrogenase 1 were under-expressed in females.
summaries.population: Prenatally exposed rat offspring

summaries.title: Quantile regression to examine the association of air pollution with subclinical atherosclerosis in an adolescent population.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Dutch PIAMA birth cohort
summaries.summary: Cross-sectional study using quantile regression to examine the association between air pollution (NO2, PM2.5, PM2.5 absorbance, PMcoarse, and ultrafine particles) and carotid intima-media thickness (CIMT) in adolescents.
key_findings: Not provided in the text snippet
summaries.population: 363 adolescents (age 16 years)
limitations: Results not provided in the snippet

summaries.title: Cognitive and Behavioral Development of 9-Year-Old Children After Maternal Cancer During Pregnancy: A Prospective Multicenter Cohort Study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used a neurocognitive test battery and parent-report behavioral questionnaires rather than EHR.
summaries.summary: A multicenter cohort study evaluating the long-term cognitive and behavioral outcomes of 9-year-old children prenatally exposed to maternal cancer and its treatment.
key_findings: Mean cognitive and behavioral outcomes were within normal ranges.
Gestational age at birth showed a positive association with Full Scale Intelligence Quotient (FSIQ).
No difference in FSIQ was found between treatment types.
Associations were observed with preterm birth, maternal death, and maternal education.
summaries.population: 151 children (mean age 9.3 years) prenatally exposed to maternal cancer
summaries.exposure_domain: maternal cancer and treatment during pregnancy

summaries.title: Evaluating Demographic Data to Improve Confidence in Equity Analytics in a Children's Hospital.
summaries.ehr_evidence: the electronic health record team standardized race and ethnicity fields... all fields are sourced from the same electronic health record field.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of demographic data collection, analysis, and visualization infrastructure to improve health equity analytics at Akron Children’s Hospital.
key_findings: Fields were not consistently accessible across reporting tools.
All fields were sourced from the same electronic health record field when present.
Identified inconsistencies in demographic data visualization.
summaries.population: Pediatric patients at Akron Children’s Hospital
summaries.exposure_domain: Demographic data and social determinants of health (SDOH)
limitations: limitations to a seldom-populated Race 2 field

summaries.title: Adverse Events Following Immunization Among Children Under Two Years of Age: A Prospective Observational Study From North India.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective community-based observational study using home visits, not EHR.
summaries.summary: A prospective community-based observational study assessing the incidence and types of adverse events following immunization (AEFI) in children under two years of age in Uttarakhand, North India.
key_findings: Incidence of AEFIs was 33.0 per 100 doses.
Most common AEFI was fever (47.6%) followed by swelling (25.0%).
All reported AEFIs were minor; no serious AEFIs were identified.
summaries.population: 255 children under two years of age in Uttarakhand, India

summaries.title: Evaluation of SARS-CoV-2 vaccination in pregnant and breastfeeding women.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used an online questionnaire to inquire about safety experiences.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of the safety profile of SARS-CoV-2 vaccines in pregnant and breastfeeding women.
key_findings: Breastfeeding women experienced higher rates of adverse events (AEs) such as fatigue, headache, and myalgia compared to pregnant women.
No pattern of serious AEs emerged.
93.1% of pregnancies were carried to term.
Vaccine efficacy was 96.3%.
summaries.population: 2192 pregnant or breastfeeding women

summaries.title: The exposome in respiratory diseases: multiple preventable risk factors from early life to adulthood.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The studies reviewed use the INMA, RHINESSA, and NutriNet-Santé cohorts, which are population-based cohorts.
summaries.summary: A journal club review of three studies investigating the impact of environmental exposures (phthalates, air pollution, and exposome profiles) on respiratory health outcomes from early life to adulthood.
key_findings: Regulation and reduction of phthalates and air pollution are supported.
Risk reduction requires a multi-interventional approach targeting early-life factors and healthy lifestyles in adulthood.
summaries.population: Children and adults
limitations: Exposome research in respiratory diseases is still in its infancy

summaries.title: Memory-like innate response to booster vaccination with MF-59 adjuvanted influenza vaccine in children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No mention of EHR or linked health data; study uses transcriptional responses from peripheral blood.
summaries.summary: Systems-level analysis of immune responses to the trivalent inactivated influenza vaccine adjuvanted with MF-59 in children (15–24 months old) and young, healthy adults.
key_findings: Primary vaccination induced a persistent transcriptional signature of innate immunity.
summaries.population: Children (15–24 months old) and young, healthy adults
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Parental, gestational, and early-life exposure to indoor environmental hazardous factors on allergic rhinitis among preschool children in Urumqi City: a case-control study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used parental reports and a case-control survey design rather than electronic health records.
summaries.summary: A case-control study investigating the association between early-life indoor environmental exposures (maternal exposure during pregnancy and child's first year) and allergic rhinitis in preschool children in Urumqi City, China.
key_findings: Cesarean section (OR = 1.31)
Father with AR (OR = 2.67)
Mother with AR (OR = 3.70)
Mother with asthma (OR = 3.11)
Mother with newly purchased furniture during pregnancy (OR = 1.49)
captured_features: Indoor environmental correlates of maternal exposure
summaries.population: Preschool children in Urumqi City, China
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets generated and/or analyzed during the current study are not publicly available due to the data belonging to the School of Public Health of Fudan University but are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.

summaries.title: Updated report of COVID-19 vaccine safety monitoring in Japan: booster doses for Omicron variants and vaccinations for infants and young children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Japanese systems for COVID-19 vaccines safety monitoring; individual case safety reports from healthcare professionals and the marketing authorisation holder (MAH). Reports from healthcare professionals and MAH (Marketing Authorization Holder). The report uses spontaneous reporting data from the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare of Japan. None
summaries.summary: Safety monitoring report of COVID-19 vaccines in Japan, including booster doses for Omicron variants and vaccinations for infants and young children.
key_findings: Confirmed cases of myocarditis and pericarditis following Pfizer vaccines for Omicron BA.1 and BA.4/BA.5 variants.
39 cases of myocarditis and 18 cases of pericarditis for Pfizer booster doses.
No strongly suspected myocarditis/pericarditis in infants and young children.
Only 1 death with a suggested causal relationship to Pfizer vaccine.
Reporting rates of serious adverse events (myocarditis/pericarditis, anaphylaxis, and death) were generally not higher than those for any booster doses.
The reporting rate of serious adverse events was low in children aged 6 months to 4 years.
summaries.population: Japanese infants (6 months-4 years), children (5-11 years), and adults (>=12 years)
limitations: Underestimation of the numbers and incidence rates of adverse events by using the spontaneous reporting data.

summaries.title: Laboratory response to an infant with suspected measles vaccine associated fever and rash in Sri Lanka.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a laboratory-based investigation using blood and throat swabs, not EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: Laboratory investigation of an infant in Sri Lanka presenting with fever and rash following a measles-containing vaccine.
key_findings: Measles vaccine type A (Edmonston-strain) was detected in the throat swab.
Influenza A RNA was detected in the throat swab.
ParvoB19 IgM was detected in the blood sample.
summaries.population: One immunocompetent child (infant) in Sri Lanka

summaries.title: Adverse event following immunization or vaccination in children in Minas Gerais: 2015 to 2020.
summaries.ehr_evidence: based on event notifications available in the National Immunization Program Information System
summaries.summary: Ecological study describing immunization errors and adverse events in children in Minas Gerais, Brazil, from 2015 to 2020.
key_findings: 3,259 immunization errors recorded (0.008% of total doses)
91.86% of errors did not result in adverse events
Most frequent error: application outside recommended age (29.12%)
Fever was the most common manifestation (40.85%)
summaries.population: children aged 0-9 years in Minas Gerais, Brazil

summaries.title: Acute appendicitis in a patient immunised with COVID-19 vaccine: A case report with morphological analysis.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the manuscript is a single case report.
summaries.summary: A case report describing a 58-year-old female who presented with acute appendicitis approximately 48 hours after receiving the first dose of the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine.
key_findings: Acute appendicitis occurred 48 hours after the first dose of the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine.
Histological analysis revealed Th-1 driven granulomatous inflammation.
summaries.population: 58-year-old female

summaries.title: COVID-19 Vaccination Responses with Different Vaccine Platforms in Patients with Inborn Errors of Immunity.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used a clinical cohort and laboratory assays (ELISA, ELISPOT, flow cytometry) rather than EHR data.
summaries.summary: Evaluation of the safety and immunogenicity of various COVID-19 vaccine platforms in patients with inborn errors of immunity (IEI) in Argentina.
key_findings: No moderate/severe vaccine-associated adverse events were observed.
Anti-S/RBD titers and neutralizing antibodies were significantly lower in IEI patients compared to healthy controls.
Moderna vaccines elicited a stronger humoral response in IEI patients.
84.5% of IEI patients showed positive S-specific IFN-γ response.
summaries.population: 118 adults and pediatric IEI patients and 37 age-matched healthy controls in Argentina

summaries.title: Open-label phase I/II clinical trial of SARS-CoV-2 receptor binding domain-tetanus toxoid conjugate vaccine (FINLAY-FR-2) in combination with receptor binding domain-protein vaccine (FINLAY-FR-1A) in children.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a clinical trial, not using EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A phase I/II clinical trial evaluating the safety and immunogenicity of a heterologous vaccination scheme (FINLAY-FR-2 and FINLAY-FR-1A) in children aged 3-18 in Havana, Cuba.
key_findings: Local pain was the only adverse event > 10%
Two doses of FINLAY-FR-2 elicited a humoral immune response similar to natural infection
Third dose with FINLAY-FR-1A increased the response in all children
summaries.population: 350 children 3-18 years old in Havana, Cuba
limitations: Limited to a specific geographic location

summaries.title: Associations of longitudinal BMI percentile classification patterns in early childhood with neighborhood-level social determinants of health.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We extracted EHR data from 2012-2019 for a children’s health system that includes 2 hospitals and wide network of outpatient clinics spanning 5 East Coast states in the US.
summaries.summary: Longitudinal analysis of BMI-percentile patterns in children aged 0-7 years and their association with neighborhood-level social determinants of health (SDOH) using EHR data.
key_findings: Five BMI-percentile classification groups identified: always obesity, overweight most of the time, increasing, decreasing, and always normal weight.
Children in the first three groups were more likely to live in neighborhoods with higher poverty, unemployment, crowded households, single-parent households, and lower preschool enrollment.
summaries.study_design: longitudinal clustering and multinomial logistic regression
summaries.population: 36,910 children aged 0-7 years in 5 East Coast states, US
limitations: Not specified in the provided text

summaries.title: Correction: Neonatal blood lead concentration predicts medium term lead-related outcomes in children ≤5 years old with congenital lead poisoning: A retrospective cohort study in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None mentioned in the correction notice.
summaries.summary: Correction notice for a retrospective cohort study on neonatal blood lead concentration and medium-term outcomes in children <5 years old in Northern Nigeria.
summaries.population: children <5 years old with congenital lead poisoning in Northern Nigeria

summaries.title: Association between prenatal exposure to alkylphenols and intelligence quotient among preschool children: sex-specific effects.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used the Guangxi Zhuang Birth Cohort, which is a prospective birth cohort, not an EHR or linked health data source.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between prenatal exposure to alkylphenols (APs) and the intelligence quotient (IQ) of preschool children, exploring sex-specific and nonmonotonic effects.
key_findings: Prenatal exposure to NP and 4-T-OP (second tertile) showed an inverse association with full-scale IQ (FSIQ) and working memory index (WMI).
Prenatal exposure to 4-N-NP (third tertile) showed a positive association with the fluid reasoning index in total children and girls.
A U-shaped relationship was observed between maternal 4-T-OP and WMI in total children and girls.
Combined AP mixtures above the 50th percentile exhibited an inverse trend on FSIQ in total children and girls.
captured_features: Maternal serum concentrations of NP, 4-T-OP, 4-N-NP, and 4-n-octylphenol
Childhood IQ scores (FSIQ, WMI, fluid reasoning index)
summaries.population: 221 mother-child pairs from the Guangxi Zhuang Birth Cohort

summaries.title: Activated fibroblasts modify keratinocyte stem niche through TET1 and IL-6 to promote their rapid transformation in a mouse model of prenatal arsenic exposure.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is a mouse model.
summaries.summary: The study examines how prenatal arsenic exposure leads to skin tumorigenesis in mouse offspring by activating dermal fibroblasts to create a pro-tumorigenic niche for keratinocyte stem cells.
key_findings: Prenatal arsenic exposure increases epidermal stem cell proliferation and skin tumorigenesis.
As-conditioned dermal fibroblasts show increased activation markers and IL-6 secretion.
IL-6 promotes epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EMT) in keratinocyte stem cells via JAK2-STAT3 signaling.
summaries.population: BALB/c mouse offspring

summaries.title: Genetic exploration of Dravet syndrome: two case report.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a case series based on clinical evaluation and genetic testing of two patients.
summaries.summary: Genetic exploration of Dravet syndrome in two Indonesian children using next-generation sequencing to identify mutations in SCN1A and other genes.
key_findings: First patient had heterozygous deletion in TTC21B, SCN1A, and SCN9A.
Second patient had a previously unreported mutation in SCN1A.
Severity of clinical manifestations correlated with the severity of genetic mutations.
summaries.population: Two Indonesian children

summaries.title: Comparison of 1 Versus 2 Doses of Quadrivalent Influenza Vaccine in 3-8-Year-Old Children with Different Immunological States - Jiangsu Province, China, 2021.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None; the study is an open-label, self-paired clinical trial using direct data collection (blood samples and monitoring).
summaries.summary: A clinical trial evaluating the immunogenicity and safety of 1 vs 2 doses of quadrivalent influenza vaccine (QIV) in children aged 3–8 years in China, stratified by prior vaccination history.
key_findings: Single dose of QIV is sufficient for children 3–8 years who have previously received 2+ doses of influenza vaccine.
Children with no prior vaccination should still receive two doses.
captured_features: HI antibody titers (GMT, GMFI, SPR, SCR)
summaries.population: healthy children aged 3–8 years in Jiangsu Province, China

summaries.title: The Epidemiological Characteristics and Spatiotemporal Clustering of Measles - China, 2005-2022.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We utilized data from the China Disease Prevention and Control Information System...
summaries.summary: Spatiotemporal analysis of measles epidemiology in China from 2005 to 2022 to identify trends and clusters.
key_findings: 732,218 measles cases reported between 2005 and 2022
Incidence rate reached a historical low of 0.039 per 100,000 in 2022
High-incidence cluster identified from 2005 to 2008 in western, central, and northern regions
Low-incidence cluster detected from 2016 to 2022 in southern and central provinces
summaries.study_design: Spatiotemporal scanning analysis and spatial autocorrelation
summaries.population: Children in China (2005-2022)

summaries.title: Association between prenatal glucocorticoid exposure and adolescent neurodevelopment: An observational follow-up study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective observational follow-up study using clinical measurements rather than EHR or linked health data.
summaries.summary: A prospective observational follow-up study investigating the long-term effects of prenatal betamethasone exposure on neurodevelopmental outcomes in adolescents.
key_findings: No significant differences in global IQ, ADHD symptoms, metabolic markers, HPAA, or ANS activity.
Reduced electrocortical activity in the frontal brain region in the betamethasone group.
summaries.population: 21 adolescents (14-15 years old) exposed to prenatal betamethasone and 35 reference children

summaries.title: Newborn glomerular function and gestational particulate air pollution.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study uses the ENVIRONAGE cohort and a Dutch questionnaire rather than EHR data.
summaries.summary: The study investigates the impact of gestational particulate air pollution (black carbon and PM2.5) on newborn glomerular function, using cord blood cystatin C as a biomarker.
key_findings: Gestational exposure to black carbon (BC) and fine particulate matter (PM2.5) was associated with increased cord blood cystatin C levels (p < 0.01).
The strongest associations were observed from the third trimester onwards.
summaries.population: 1484 mother-child pairs in Genk, Belgium

summaries.title: Associations between gestational exposure to perfluoroalkyl substances, fetal growth, and the mediation effect of thyroid hormones.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Ezhou birth cohort study, which is a cohort study, not an EHR.
summaries.summary: The study investigated the association between maternal serum perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFASs) and neonatal growth metrics, specifically examining the mediating role of maternal thyroid hormones.
key_findings: PFNA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth length.
PFOA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth head circumference.
Maternal free triiodothyronine (FT3) mediated 36.7% of the negative association between PFNA and birth length.
Maternal free thyroxine (FT4) mediated 30.8% of the effect of PFOA on head circumference.
captured_features: Maternal thyroid hormone levels (FT3, FT4, TSH, TT4, TT3, TG)
summaries.population: 374 mother-infant pairs from the Ezhou birth cohort study (2019-2020)
summaries.data_availability_statement: The datasets used and/or analysed during the current study available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.

summaries.title: Early Life Exposure to Manure-Fertilized Soil Shapes the Gut Antibiotic Resistome.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None. The study is an experimental animal study using mice.
summaries.summary: Experimental study investigating how early life exposure to manure-fertilized soil shapes the gut antibiotic resistome in mice.
key_findings: Manure fertilization enriched tetracycline-resistant ARGs in both red and black soils.
Mice in these environments harbored markedly higher abundances of ARGs, particularly the tet(Q) gene.
Muribaculaceae and Bacteroidaceae were identified as key hosts of tet(Q).
summaries.population: Mice
summaries.data_availability_statement: Data Availability Statement

summaries.title: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors.
summaries.ehr_evidence: No EHR used; data were collected from 644 fecal samples and direct host/exposome measurements from wild and zoo-housed bonobos.
summaries.summary: A comprehensive atlas of the bonobo gut bacteriome and its associated host and exposome factors, comparing wild and zoo-housed populations to understand the effects of industrialization and social contact.
key_findings: Clear differences in gut bacterial diversity and composition between wild and captive bonobos
Social contact is a stronger predictor of bacteriome similarity than genetics or other exposome factors
Maternal effects persist even in non-cohabiting mother-offspring dyads
summaries.data_source_type: Primary data collection (fecal samples and host/exposome data)
summaries.population: 212 wild and zoo-housed bonobos (Pan paniscus)
summaries.exposure_domain: Exposome (diet, transit time, early life adversity, medication use, social contact, maternal effects)
summaries.data_availability_statement: deposited at the European Nucleotide Archive under project ENA: PRJEB86099 and are publicly available as of the date of publication

summaries.title: Using machine learning to predict future foster care admission.
summaries.ehr_evidence: We use claims data for children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio as well as for linked adults, along with data on individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH) factors.
summaries.summary: The study evaluates the use of machine learning (gradient-boosted trees) to predict future foster care admissions in children by utilizing Medicaid claims data and social determinants of health (SDOH) factors.
key_findings: The gradient-boosted tree model identified 2408 children (1.32%) at risk of foster care admission.
The model outperformed logistic regression.
The inclusion of SDOH data significantly improved predictive accuracy, increasing the positive predictive value (PPV) from 27.44% to 84.72%.
captured_features: individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH) factors
summaries.population: Children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio
limitations: Ethical and legal concerns regarding the use of such tools for selecting families for child welfare investigations
SDOH data was only available for 85% of the children

summaries.title: Moderate prenatal alcohol exposure differentially alters acute ethanol sensitivity of GABAergic transmission in CRFR1- and CRFR1+ CeM Neurons.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: Laboratory study investigating how moderate prenatal alcohol exposure (PAE) alters acute ethanol's modulation of GABAergic neurotransmission in the central amygdala (CeM) of adult rats.
key_findings: PAE alters acute EtOH modulation of GABA transmission in a cell-type and concentration-dependent manner.
Effects are sex-specific in adult offspring.
summaries.population: Adult male and female rats (P80-105)

summaries.title: Sugar restriction in the first 1000 days after conception, and long-term respiratory health: a quasi-experiment study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Outcomes included incident asthma, COPD, and IPF (ascertained by health records)
summaries.summary: A quasi-experimental study investigating the long-term impact of early-life sugar restriction (based on the 1953 UK sugar rationing) on adult respiratory health and lung function using UK Biobank data.
key_findings: Early-life sugar rationing associated with lower risks of asthma (HR 0.75) and COPD (HR 0.73).
Delayed disease onset by ≤ 3.6 years.
Rationed participants showed higher mean FEV1%, FVC%, and FEV1/FVC ratio.
Diabetes and hypertension mediated 18.2% of the effect.
captured_features: demographic, socioeconomic, lifestyle, and clinical factors
summaries.population: 58,670 UK Biobank participants born 1951–1956
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data are accessible through a public, open-access repository . Data from the UKB, ELSA, and HRS are available to researchers on request.

summaries.title: Impact of maternal fasting blood glucose in pregnancy on body composition, anthropometric, and metabolic outcomes in newborns and 3-Year-Old offspring: results from the lifestyle in pregnancy and offspring study.
summaries.ehr_evidence: The analysis used data from the Lifestyle in Pregnancy (LiP) study and its 3-year follow-up, the LiPO study.
summaries.summary: Study investigating whether maternal fasting glucose predicts offspring body composition, anthropometry, and metabolic outcomes at birth and at 3 years.
key_findings: Higher maternal fasting glucose was associated with a higher birth weight z-score.
Glucose ≥ 5.6 mmol/L predicted higher birth weight.
No significant associations were found for BMI z-score or metabolic markers at 3 years.
summaries.population: 301 women in LiP study, 157 in LiPO study, 75 children with DXA assessments
limitations: Small sample size for DXA assessments (n=75)
summaries.data_availability_statement: The data that support the findings of this study are available from C.A.V., but restrictions apply to their availability. The data were used under license for the current study and include unpublished results that are still being analyzed. Therefore, they are not publicly available. However, data may be obtained from the corresponding author on reasonable request and with permission from C.A.V.

summaries.title: Coverage and associated factors of inactivated polio vaccine uptake among children aged 12-23 months in Sub-Saharan Africa.
summaries.ehr_evidence: Data from the recent demographic and health survey (DHS) in 20 SSA countries.
summaries.summary: Assessment of inactivated polio vaccine (IPV) coverage and its determinants among children aged 12–23 months in 20 Sub-Saharan African countries.
key_findings: Pooled IPV coverage was 65.01%
Significant positive determinants: maternal education, media exposure, antenatal/postnatal care, urban residence
Inverse association with rich household wealth
summaries.population: Children aged 12–23 months in 20 Sub-Saharan African countries

summaries.title: Maternal exposure to cyclophosphamide during prepubertal life does not affect the cryosusceptibility of in vitro derived mouse embryos.
summaries.ehr_evidence: None
summaries.summary: A mouse model study evaluating the impact of prepubertal cyclophosphamide (CY) exposure on the cryosusceptibility of embryos derived via in vitro fertilization (IVF).
key_findings: No significant differences were found between CY-exposed and control groups in terms of post-warming survival or pluripotency marker expression.
summaries.population: Mouse model
